brewing_methods.name: V60 Pour-Over
brewing_methods.description: A manual pour-over method using a conical dripper. It emphasizes clarity of flavor and highlights subtle acidic notes.
brewing_methods.tech_details: Requires a paper filter. Grind size: Medium-Fine. Water temp: 92-94C.

brewing_methods.name: French Press
brewing_methods.description: An immersion brewing method that produces a heavy, full-bodied cup with rich texture.
brewing_methods.tech_details: Metal mesh filter allows more oils into the cup. Grind size: Coarse. Steeping time: 4 minutes.

brewing_methods.name: AeroPress
brewing_methods.description: A versatile pressure-based brewer that can make anything from espresso-style concentrates to clean filter-like coffee.
brewing_methods.tech_details: Highly portable. Grind size: Fine to Medium. Fast brew time (90 seconds).

brewing_methods.name: Cold Brew
brewing_methods.description: Coffee grounds steeped in cold water for a long period (12-24 hours). Results in low acidity and high sweetness.
brewing_methods.tech_details: Concentrated caffeine. Best served over ice.

coffee_science.facts: Caffeine blocks adenosine receptors in the brain. Peak effects occur within 30-60 minutes.
coffee_science.low_caffeine_tips: Swiss Water process decaf removes 99.9% of caffeine without chemicals.

coffee_science.facts: Medium-roasted arabica beans have natural sweetness. Adding dairy can mask bitterness due to lactose and fat content.
coffee_science.health_tip: For low calories, try oat milk or drinking coffee black to enjoy the origin notes.

coffee_origins.profile: Floral, berry-like, and highly acidic. Often considered the birthplace of coffee.
Well-balanced, nutty, and chocolatey. Versatile for both black and milk-based drinks.
Earthy, spicy, and very low acidity. Heavy body.
